cards.back: A Local Area Network (LAN) that uses Ethernet to connect devices and allow data transfer between them
The amount of data that can be sent in a single packet by a node before the network becomes congested

cards.front: How many nodes can be used in an Ethernet LAN?
cards.back: Any number of nodes (n) can be used in an Ethernet LAN

cards.front: What is the purpose of setting multiple traffic nodes?
cards.back: To distribute traffic across the network and avoid congestion

cards.front: Why is it important to plot congestion window for different source/destination?
cards.back: To visualize the amount of data being sent and received between nodes and identify potential bottlenecks
